title: Tuned Freight-Yard Sorting
seed_problem: balanced max-k-cut with per-part modular tonnage residues and a size balance band
mutation: composite: multiply each track's max-cut boundary value by a residue-tuning bonus (1+lam) when its tonnage sum hits the target residue mod m, under a size balance band; nonlinear product couples cut and number-theoretic tuning
strategies: round-robin baseline: car i -> ((i-1) mod k)+1, feasible band, zero tuned tracks
cut-only hill climb: size-preserving swaps that increase total separated conflict weight, ignoring residues
full-objective local search: size-preserving swaps optimizing F, biased to swap light (edge-free) adjuster cars to tune high-boundary tracks while separating heavy conflicts
planted/needle exploitation: detect the hidden high-cut partition or the heavy needle track and tune specifically its residue for the lam multiplier
